title: [7] MCP Server Phase 5 — Wiring + Packaging
why_we_need_this: Phase 4 (task #5, completed) plus its same-day bug fix (task #6) proved all 19 MCP tools work correctly against the live dashboard over the real WireGuard tunnel (`project-context/routes/mcp-server/README.md` L4-5). Phase 5 is the final phase of the owner-approved 5-phase roadmap: turn the validated, working binary into something actually callable from the operator's day-to-day MCP host, and leave a documented path for reusing this pattern on a future project. Without this phase the MCP server exists as a validated binary but nothing wires it into an actual MCP host session.
acceptance_criteria:
- Read `project-context/routes/mcp-server/README.md` in full (current STATUS, all resolved decisions: wrapper-only architecture, stdio transport, single hardcoded-with-override target `172.16.15.1:8080`, laptop-side placement, no Docker, in-repo `mcp/` placement, confirmation-gate mechanics) plus `mcp/docs/tool-surface.md`, `mcp/docs/confirmation-gates.md`, and the Phase 4 validation results doc under `mcp/docs/` to confirm exactly which 19 tools ship and their final names/signatures. Use `search_project_context` to cross-check — do not derive the tool list solely from re-reading the Go source.
- Build/confirm a release build process for the `mcp/` Go module producing a single static binary (document the exact build command, e.g. `go build -o mcp-server ./cmd/mcp-server`, under `mcp/README.md` or similar — no new build tooling/CI, this repo has no CI per CLAUDE.md).
- Write a concrete MCP host config example (e.g. a `mcpServers` JSON block suitable for Claude Code / Claude Desktop) showing how to point the host at the built binary, including how to set the `MCP_DASHBOARD_ADDR` env var/flag override established in Phase 2. Place this example in `mcp/README.md`.
- Document, in the same `mcp/README.md`, how to adapt this exact pattern to a different project's WireGuard tunnel IP (per the owner's stated usage pattern of running one MCP server per project) — i.e. what specifically needs to change (target address) versus what stays identical (transport, no-Docker, tool set), so this build is a repeatable template rather than a one-off.
- Confirm no Docker, no always-on HTTP/SSE listener is introduced anywhere in this task — packaging must remain a stdio-spawned subprocess consistent with Phases 1-4.
- Do not add any application-layer auth — the no-auth-at-the-dashboard-boundary model remains a settled, owner-accepted risk; this task is packaging/wiring only.
- This task closes the roadmap: once complete, the overall MCP server initiative (Phases 1-5) should read as DONE, not just individual phases.
important_constraints:
- This is Phase 5 of the approved 5-phase roadmap — wiring and packaging only. Do not add new tools, do not change any tool behavior, do not touch the confirmation-gate design (resolved in Phase 3).
- Do not modify `dashboard/` or `terraform/` — this task only adds/extends files under `mcp/` (README/docs + build instructions).
- No Docker, no always-on listener — stdio subprocess transport only, per every prior phase's constraint.
- Do not edit anything under `project-context/` directly — that's Praxis-managed via `update_project_context`, out of scope for this task.
- Follow this repo's git conventions once work is committed: PR-based workflow, `feat:` commit prefix (or `docs:` if the change is purely documentation — check whether any build script/config file is added when deciding), `feat/`- or `docs/`-prefixed branch name accordingly.
- After this task's work lands, run `update_project_context` so the mcp-server route's STATUS line reflects Phase 5 as complete and the overall MCP server roadmap (Phases 1-5) as DONE.
